basics.name: Amirhossein Ravari
basics.label: PhD Candidate · Reinforcement Learning, Inverse RL, LLMs
basics.summary: Machine learning researcher specializing in reinforcement learning and human–AI collaboration, with 5+ years designing and deploying deep learning solutions. Skilled in inverse and multi-agent RL, large language models, and applied ML across healthcare, cybersecurity, and disaster response.

work.name: Northeastern University
work.summary: Imani Lab, Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering.
highlights: Developed inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) and multi-agent RL frameworks for modeling human–AI collaboration in complex decision-making, leading to publications in IEEE TAI, ACC, L4DC, and Asilomar.
Designed adversarial inverse learning methods conditioned on human psychological factor models to improve cyber defense policy optimization.
Formulated optimal inference algorithms for hidden Markov models from expert-acquired data, published in IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence.
Built hybrid probabilistic models for heterogeneous human teams operating under uncertainty and partial observability.

work.name: Northeastern University
work.summary: Reinforcement Learning and Decision Making under Uncertainty (graduate course).
highlights: Teaching and mentoring graduate students; supporting course design, grading, exam preparation, and weekly office hours.

work.name: Walkky
work.summary: Multimodal language models for medical time-series and imaging data.
highlights: Researched multimodal language models integrating ECG signals, echocardiograms, and clinical reports for unified diagnostic representations.
Contributed to generative self-supervised models for medical time-series using structured tokenization and masked prediction objectives.
Implemented Vision Transformer (ViT) models for ECG image classification, achieving 82% accuracy with bagging and majority-voting ensembles.

work.name: Sharif University of Technology
work.summary: Multi-agent RL, deep generative models, and EEG classification.
highlights: Developed multi-agent RL frameworks in partially observable environments using MDPs and Nash equilibria for emergent self-assembly tasks.
Implemented CNN–RNN architectures and GANs in PyTorch for synthetic data generation and automated image captioning.
Designed LSTM-based sequence-to-sequence models for culturally adaptive text generation.
Built EEG-based classifiers using statistical machine learning to detect emotion states from visual stimuli.
Developed parallel C++/CUDA GPU-accelerated algorithms for nearest-neighbor histogram computations.

work.name: Sharif University of Technology
work.summary: Object-Oriented Programming, Analog Circuits Lab, Principles of Electronics.
highlights: Head Teaching Assistant for Object-Oriented Programming, mentoring 150+ students and coordinating labs and projects.

awards.title: ECE Scholars Fellowship
awards.summary: Selective doctoral fellowship awarded by the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

awards.title: Experiential PhD Leadership Certificate
awards.summary: Leadership development program for doctoral students.
